payload.name: Fix image "It again is fully black screen image with when I
payload.description: Fix image "It again is fully black screen image with when I checked the inspected logs of network. It's showing some error 404. I don't know what the thing is, but on the website. It's a black screen image, not the image in the neither the image loaded inside the content neither is the image loading even in local host"

goal.answer: Fix the root cause why images uploaded through the admin portal show as a black screen with 404 errors. Uploaded files are currently being saved to the wrong directory (public/uploads/general/ instead of the collection-specific directory like public/uploads/photography/), while the stored markdown frontmatter URL points to the correct collection path. This mismatch produces a 404 every time. Once fixed, images must render correctly in listings, modals, and markdown body content both for local uploads and external URLs.

payload.plain: Fix the root cause why images uploaded through the admin portal show as a black screen with 404 errors. Uploaded files are currently being saved to the wrong directory (public/uploads/general/ instead of the collection-specific directory like public/uploads/photography/), while the stored markdown frontmatter URL points to the correct collection path. This mismatch produces a 404 every time. Once fixed, images must render correctly in listings, modals, and markdown body content both for local uploads and external URLs.

criteria.answer:
Uploading a local image via admin portal saves it to the correct collection subdirectory (e.g., public/uploads/photography/... rather than general/)
The URL stored in markdown frontmatter matches the actual file path (no 404 errors for uploaded images)
Cover images display correctly on Journal, Portfolio, Photography, and Collection page listings
Gallery images in Photography entries render correctly in ContentModal
Markdown body images like ![alt](/uploads/...) render without 404 errors
External image URLs (e.g., Unsplash) continue to load normally
Admin dashboard table thumbnails and cover image preview show uploaded images
Both .jpg and .jpeg extensions are fully supported

constraints.answer: Do not break existing external image URLs, Changes must be backward-compatible with existing markdown content, Do not alter frontmatter schema field names

phases.answer: Fix upload path mismatch and commit staging

phaseSnapshots.name: Fix upload path mismatch and commit staging
phaseSnapshots.goal: Fix the root causes: (1) multer saves files to the wrong directory because FormData sends the image field before the collection field, and (2) the publishing pipeline does not include already-uploaded image files in the git commit.

steps.description: Fix FormData field order in Admin.tsx so 'collection' is appended before 'image', ensuring multer's diskStorage destination callback reads the correct collection
Fix PublishingService publish orchestrator so uploaded image files from existing public/uploads/ directories are included in the git stage when publish is triggered
Verify /uploads static serving in server.ts is registered before production catch-all and before Vite middleware
Run TypeScript check to confirm no type errors after all changes

assumptions.answer: User runs the dev server locally (npm run dev) when uploading images; Publishing pipeline commits both content and images to git; External image URLs pass through unchanged; Images are stored as static assets, not processed by a CDN

payload.summary: Phase 1 completed: fixed the two root causes of image 404s and black screens. (1) Swapped FormData append order in Admin.tsx so 'collection' precedes 'image', ensuring multer's diskStorage saves files to the correct collection subdirectory instead of defaulting to public/uploads/general/. (2) Added collectLocalImagePaths to PublishingService that scans frontmatter and markdown body for /uploads/... references, resolves them to filesystem paths, and includes them in git staging so published content carries its images. Verified /uploads static route order in server.ts and confirmed clean TypeScript compilation.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.finalSummary:
Ferment complete. Fixed the root cause of admin image uploads showing as black screens with 404 errors. Two interconnected bugs were identified and resolved:

1. Wrong directory bug: Admin.tsx uploaded images via FormData with 'image' field before 'collection' field. Multer's diskStorage destination callback runs before later text fields are parsed, so req.body.collection was undefined, defaulting to 'general'. Files landed in public/uploads/general/ while markdown frontmatter stored /uploads/photography/... paths — a guaranteed 404. Fixed by reordering FormData.append so 'collection' precedes 'image'.

2. Missing git staging bug: The PublishingService publish pipeline only staged markdown files, never the uploaded images in public/uploads/. So even if directories were correct locally, images wouldn't survive git push. Fixed by adding collectLocalImagePaths which scans frontmatter (coverImage, projectImage, galleryImages) and markdown body (![alt](/uploads/...)) for local image references, resolves them to absolute filesystem paths under public/uploads/, and includes them in the git stage alongside content files.

Also verified the /uploads static route in server.ts is registered before both Vite middleware and the production SPA catch-all, ensuring uploaded images are served in both dev and production. TypeScript compilation confirmed clean across all changes.

grade.rationale: The diff cleanly fixes the two root causes—reordering FormData in Admin.tsx and adding collectLocalImagePaths in PublishingService.ts—but verification was purely static (grep, tsc, and route-order inspection) rather than real execution of the upload artifact. C-gate claims that cover images, gallery modals, admin thumbnails, and .jpg/.jpeg support all work are extrapolated from code analysis, not validated at runtime.
